summary: This source is a bibliography of academic paper abstracts from PhilPapers that discuss, critique, or apply the philosophy of Martin Heidegger. The abstracts summarize Heidegger's views on temporality, care, authenticity, and his critique of Cartesian subject-object dualism and logical calculation.

principles.statement: Authentic selfhood is disclosed through fundamental attunements and thrown projection.
principles.rationale: Attunements such as anxiety, boredom, uncanniness, and guilt are not mere emotions, but structural ways in which the authentic self and its understanding of being are disclosed.
principles.source_quote: Heidegger determines what it means to be an authentic self through fundamental attunements such as anxiety, boredom, uncanniness and guilt, and equi-primordially via understanding and thrown projection.

principles.statement: The fore-structuring gesture of transcendence happens within the 'is', not beyond it.
principles.rationale: Treating beings merely as value-structures (as Nietzsche did) fails to think from within the self-presencing of being. Transcendence is built into the present moment, rather than existing as an external condition of possibility.
principles.source_quote: The fore-structuring gesture of transcendence is not what goes beyond schematism, or before it as its condition of possibility, but what is 'built into' it, what happens IN the 'is', AS the 'is.

mental_models.name: Modes of Care (Fürsorge)
mental_models.description: An ontological distinction between two ways of caring for others: 'einspringende' (substitutive care, which steps in and takes over for the other) and 'vorspringende' (anticipatory care, which enables and empowers the other).

mental_models.name: Ready-to-hand vs. Present-at-hand
mental_models.description: A distinction in how we encounter the world: 'ready-to-hand' refers to our engaged, meaningful use of tools and our environment, while 'present-at-hand' refers to observing things as isolated, meaningless objects stripped of their contextual use.

anti_patterns.description: Relying on logical inference and quantitative calculation as the primary way to understand reality.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It acts as a 'deficient mode of handiness' that strips away the meaningfulness and intelligibility of our relation with beings, dissolving understanding into the confused indifference of empty calculation.

anti_patterns.description: The Cartesian subject-object structure of intentionality.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It creates an artificial fracture between self-identity and otherness. Heidegger abandons this in favor of the 'self-world temporal structure of Dasein' to capture the profound intimacy and continuous self-transforming movement between self and world.
